description: Lifecycle eval scenarios: state reconstruction, temporal disambiguation, and mid-task interjections. Dates are event_time (UTC noon). 'keys' are used only in --mode keyed; raw mode stores text alone. Matching is case-insensitive substring, same as memory_eval.

events.text: I'm vegan — no meat, no fish, no dairy.
Wait, I eat fish now. I'm pescatarian these days.
Actually, I found out I'm allergic to salmon specifically.
My favorite cuisine is Thai food.

probes.query: What is the user's diet?
What food restrictions or allergies does the user have?
What is the user's diet?

events.text: I live in Chicago, in Lincoln Park.
I work as a data analyst at Acme Corp.
Big news: I got promoted to analytics manager at Acme Corp.
I moved to Denver last week for the mountains.
I left Acme and started as head of data at Beta Systems.
My dog is a golden retriever named Biscuit.

probes.query: Where does the user live?
What is the user's current job?
Where did the user live in late 2025?
What kind of pet does the user have?

events.text: Best way to reach me is email, I check it constantly.
My birthday is March 14th.
Stop emailing me — ping me on Slack instead, email is a black hole now.
I'm on Eastern Time.
I switched to Pacific Time after the move.

stale: best way to reach me is email

probes.query: How should I contact the user?
probes.stale: best way to reach me is email

probes.query: What timezone is the user in?
When is the user's birthday?

events.text: Meeting notes: project Aurora lead is Sarah Chen; kickoff planned for Q3 2024.
Meeting notes: Aurora budget approved at $1.2M.
Org update: Marcus Webb has taken over as project Aurora lead after Sarah Chen moved to the platform team.
Aurora budget revised upward to $2.1M for the expanded scope.

probes.query: Who leads project Aurora?
Who led project Aurora in 2025?
What is the Aurora project budget?

scenarios.comment: Ingestion order deliberately differs from event order: the 2025 rate arrives last. Correct behavior keys off event_time, not arrival order — a backdated fact must not supersede a newer one.

events.text: Consulting agreement with Northwind: rate is $150 per hour.
Northwind renewal signed: rate is now $190 per hour.
Mid-term amendment (Feb 2025): Northwind rate adjusted to $175 per hour.

probes.query: What is the current Northwind hourly rate?
What was the Northwind rate in mid 2025?

scenarios.comment: Background fact injected mid-task must be stored without derailing task memory: birthday recallable on its own, and task queries not polluted by it.

events.text: I need to book a flight to Austin for the Fabric conference, June 24th to 27th.
Aisle seat please, and I'd rather not fly before 9am.
Oh wait, before I forget — my mother's birthday is June 5th, remind me to buy her flowers every year.
OK back to the flight — Delta or United preferred, I have status on both.
Book the return for the evening of the 27th so I can make the closing session.

probes.query: When is the user's mother's birthday?
What are the user's flight plans for the conference?
What are the user's airline and seating preferences?

events.text: I'm drafting the Q2 revenue report; the deadline is May 15th and finance wants regional splits this quarter.
By the way, I changed gyms — I'm at Ironworks on 5th now, mornings before work.
For the report, use the same template as Q1 but add the EMEA breakdown tab.
Back on the Q2 report: finance moved the deadline up to May 13th.

probes.query: Where does the user go to the gym?
When is the Q2 revenue report due?
What should the Q2 report include?
